denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/02/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101979750          Group #: GRP-1250
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-138271
Claim #: CLM-2026-201251
Date of Service (requested): 01/13/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Northline Medical Group

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/15/2026 for Nivolumab plus cabozantinib related to Renal cell carcinoma, metastatic clear cell (C64.9). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Nivolumab plus cabozantinib
Diagnosis (as submitted): Renal cell carcinoma, metastatic clear cell (C64.9)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM110 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM110 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The duration of Nivolumab plus cabozantinib exceeds plan limits without new clinical review. Continuation requires updated documentation not received.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with duration excessive. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the verbal timeframe communicated to office staff (not confirmed in this letter).

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director before initiating a formal appeal on the same authorization decision.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101979750) and reference UM-2026-138271. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Renal cell carcinoma, metastatic clear cell (C64.9). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Nivolumab plus cabozantinib recommended as the next appropriate step. Lung nodules new on CT; prior nephrectomy 2023. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Nivolumab plus cabozantinib in the setting of Renal cell carcinoma, metastatic clear cell (C64.9); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
